Gold Myr & Estallia
A bit of warp travel and a lot of awkward conversations later, and you arrive on Mhen’ga.

\n\nDuring the long trip, at least a few of the remnant soldiers open up to you when they catch you alone around the ship. You’re the first person outside their enclave some of them have seen for months, and the desire to talk finally overcomes them. You’re regaled with variations on the same story: their army’s defeat, their mother’s decision to continue to fight on in spite of the cease fire, and the months of scrounging through the wreckage of their world to eke out a living while striking back at the Federation’s occupying forces.

\n\nAnd the more they talk, the more excited the myr become. Several of them share their hopes for their own little tunnel-houses, or how they want to roam the surface with the native wasp-folk, or start their own mines... the possibilities seem endless, growing with each myr you speak with.

\n\nWhen you finally land at Esbeth’s starport, Estallia is holding court in your cargo hold, surrounding herself with her daughters and beginning to organize them into groups of builders and land-clearers, farmers and guards. This is the first time you’ve seen a gold myr queen actually directing her brood, watching the dozens of smaller myr listening attentively, almost as if hypnotized by their mother’s authoritative voice booming throughout the ship’s hold. The shudder of your ship dropping out of LightDrive speeds puts an end to the planning session, and the exiled queen turns to you with a genuine smile and rises to her feet, towering over the assembled myr crawling all over the hold.
\n\n“Have we arrived, captain?” she asks, going to one of the view ports overlooking the planet below. “This... this blue-green world will be our new home?”
\n\n“Welcome to Mhen’ga,” you tell her, stepping down into the hold. “What do you say we take you down planetside?”

\n\n“Thank you for this, captain,” Estallia says, reaching an arm down to your shoulder. “Truly. My daughters and I will spend a few days building a base, and from there-”

\n\nEstallia grimaces, biting her lip. “We will... build a home for ourselves. Perhaps we will see you in a few days, once we are settled.”

\n\nEstallia shakes her head sadly. “Gold myr are by our nature gentle and good-hearted, but this generation of queens has forgotten what it means to rule. \n\n“Better than even I had hoped,” Estallia answers, pride swelling in her voice. “My daughters are nothing if not industrious and hard-working women, and they’ve taken to the challenges of settling this new world with the same gusto that all the other colonists have. \n\nThe red soldiers nod their agreement, though much less enthusiastically. Their pride may be dampened from the loss, but they still manage to show a little gratitude. Moreso, once Lieve downs her cup of wine and speaks again: “So since Sellera doesn’t seem like she’s gonna be giving our starward mercenary a reward for our collective cockup, me and the girls all pitched in a little and got you something!”

\n\nShe waves over one of her wives, who approaches with a fairly large bundle held in two of her arms. Lieve takes it and presents it to you, saying, “Here. May it serve you well next time you pull the feelers of a bunch of rebels.”

\n\nYou open the bundle and roll out a heavy black overcoat, the same kind you’ve seen a lot of Federation officers wearing. The crossed hammers of the red government are proudly displayed on the shoulders, and where the unit patch would otherwise be they’ve stitched in a starfield surrounding a big, brown orb -- Myrellion itself.
\n\n“Snazzy, huh?” Lieve says, bumping you with an elbow. “Well c’mon, let’s see you in it!”

\n\nYou spend the next few minutes modelling in your Federation greatcoat for the giggling commandos, knocking back the sweet, thick honey wine as you do so. It’s been tailored more-or-less right for you, you think, and it’s not too hot or heavy down here in the caverns under Myrellion. Not bad at all... and pretty damn sexy looking. By the time you’re done, several of the myr are looking a little tipsy, and you’ve lost count of the number of times your butt’s been grabbed or somebody’s felt up your "+(pc.tone>=60&&pc.thickness>=60?"muscles":"arms")+" through your sleeves. The conversation’s died down, so you give the commandos your farewell and get underway. You’ve still got work to do of your own, after all.
\n\n

“Hey, Lieve, I got something for you,” you say, taking the bundle of remnant documents out of your pack. You hand them over to the red myr commando, who spends a minute looking them over with a critical eye.

\n\n“Huh, looks like a Republic code book. Recent, too... I heard some of these phrases on the radio just yesterday. This is a real lucky find! I’ll take this to the Intelligence girls up at the manor and get ‘em working on deciphering what we’ve been picking up from Gildenmere. With any luck, you just gave us a big leg up over the enemy!”
\n\nLieve gives you a huge grin and leans in, planting a wet kiss on your cheek and grabbing a handful of [pc.butt] while she’s leaned in close. “You’re the best alien a girl could wish for.”
\n\nLieve gives you a firm clap on the shoulder and tucks the documents under her arm. “You’re a real friend to the Federation, [pc.name]. Don’t let the Field Marshal tell you otherwise.”
\n\nWith that, Lieve squeezes past you through the bunker door and starts jogging north towards Sellera’s manor. Looks like you’ve given the Federation a big lead over the Republics!

Gold Myr & Estallia

You take a shuttle down from your ship to the coordinates of the remnant beacon, barreling through the wispy clouds over Myrellion’s southern deserts to a long salt flat that seems utterly devoid of... well, anything. Your sensors pick up a few crashed aircraft and a handful of bombed-out entrances to the underworld hives, but that’s about it. Still, you fly down to the coordinates and set ‘er down on the rolling dunes.

\n\nYou haven’t even made it to the airlock when you hear a loud thump thump, a banging on the hatch from outside. That was fast. You slide open the airlock, and sure enough, there’s a gold myr standing outside with a tattered cloak wrapped around her slender shoulders. She squints at you, shielding her black eyes from the sun, and says “You’re Steele, yes? Good. My queen mother and the remains of our warriors are coming soon. I hope, for all our sakes, you are true to your word.”

\n\nOver the next hour, dozens of gold myr arrive at your shuttle, coming in ragged trains from out of caves and hidden dugouts across the plains. You usher them aboard, helping them find seats and passing out your shuttle’s emergency rations between waves of haggard, war-weary women. You’re keenly aware of all the weapons they bring aboard, slung on shoulders or holstered on hips as they embark, held separate from the rest of their equipment.

\n\nGuess they still don’t trust you. Hard to blame them... but it doesn’t exactly ease the quiet tension in the air. Soon your shuttle’s almost completely full of remnant soldiers and crates full of food, clothing, tools, building supplies... everything a little colony of myr could want to build themselves a new life. Everything except a queen.

\n\nYou’re about to ask what gives when you finally see the towering black shadow of the War Queen emerging from over a distant dune, walking alone across the sand. Unlike your first meeting, she’s exchanged the tank treads for a simple gray tunic with a long scarf wrapping around her head and neck; her swords are sheathed on her abdomen, leaving her four hands free to each carry a metal crate under her arms.

\n\n“Offworlder,” Estallia says by way of greeting, ducking her head down low to peer into the main airlock. Several of her daughters scramble back to the cargo hold to open the only queen-sized door available. “I’m... surprised you came. It seems I’ve misjudged you yet again. One of the first times I’m happy to have been proven wrong.”

\n\nThe War Queen gives you a slight nod and pulls herself aboard, adding the heavily laden crates to the rest of her colony’s supplies. The entire ship shudders and shakes with the queen’s every step, making her daughters sway unsteadily until they find somewhere for her to sit down. Once everyone is secure, you clamber up into the cockpit and get underway, flying the former freedom fighters to their new home...

They were frail, barely more than dried sprouts good only to be ground into bland food paste. Just look at them! They’re so healthy, so lush, so full of life - Sorry. I think I’m gonna cry,” the former XO whispers, resting her fingers on a light blue sapling. Something’s not right here. The Peers don’t create, they only destroy. These plants and trees must hide a terrible secret. ] "])))

textify(Y7||(Y7=(0,Ce.Z)([" This section of the Investigative Viridiplantae Yard is wilder than the rest. Large trees and thorns pierce the walls, hugging the room’s furniture with their vibrant colors and iridescent spines. Droplets rain down from the ceiling, watering the grassy soil and phosphorescent mushrooms. [flags.EGG_EXPLORE_LAB |The charming alraune has removed her bark-covered roots from the doors, allowing you to cross her garden and continue your trip to the Main Lab. She observes you from her floral pod, lusting for your alien goods. “Paullie! [pc.name]! Can I kiss the sweet flowwie lady?” Zero Plus asks? The former XO shakes her head. “She’s had enough for today. We still need to take control of the ship, remember?” The bimbo nurse tilts her head. “Oh! Of course! Silly me. Then, we can kiss and cuddle with her all day!” [pc.isBimbo |Gosh, that’d be so nice. You could use a sexy plant lady on your ship, especially if you can sleep in her plush, velvety petals. |“Only if she behaves,” you add. An uncontrollable dryad is the last thing you need right now. ] |Long, thic[silly |c |k ] vines block the doors leading to the shortcut to the main lab. They’re covered in bark as dense as steel, surrounded with voluptuous, blood-red poppies. You won’t cut them with conventional means! “A dead end,” Dabbebieth gasps. Paulette runs her sharp finger along a verdant tentacle. “I can feel a pulse! This glade... It’s a single, organic thing! The trees are her lungs, the grass her skin, the flowers her blood, these thorns are her feet, the leaves are her ears...” Then what’s her heart? The former XO sets her eyes on the lush orchid in the middle of the pond. The huge flower spreads fragrant pollen all over the garden, filling the air with an intoxicating potency. “It’s here, without a shadow of a doubt. I’d rather not open it, if possible. Who knows what evil lurks inside that splendid shell.” The western door is blocked by vegetation. Perhaps there’s a way to clear it? ] "])))
Clear West Door

textify(M7||(M7=(0,Ce.Z)([" Many flowers, bushes, and vines decorate the alleyway. They are not as dense and vibrant as in the Investigative Viridiplantae Yard, but their resilience is impressive. There’s only a few droplets running down the pipeline that waters them from time to time, but they’re massive all the same. They are certainly from a batch designed to thrive in desert-like environments. Little by little, the alleyway becomes larger, cleaner, brighter, as if you reached the end of a tunnel. You can hear machines humming and liquids bubbling from the other side. It’s certainly the Main Lab! “Hurry up! We’re getting close to the flamy hills!” Zero Plus babbles. Indeed. You can see chains of smoking mountains from the portholes. Dark, volcanic clouds and fiery cinders surround the vessel. If you don’t act quickly, the ashes will choke the thrusters and plunge the E.G.G. right into the Crucible’s caldera. "])))

textify(G7||(G7=(0,Ce.Z)([" “Yeeeeee! Lifty lofty rides!” Zero Plus squeals, pouncing toward the elevator’s controls. You can see the Main Lab below. It’s a giant room filled with huge machines, hundreds of stasis capsules, and titanic tubes filled with developing biomass. Here and there, you spot test benches for energy weapons and power armor that would make an XO’s chestpiece look like parchment in comparison. There’s a large, circular platform in the middle of the lab. A huge throne sits amidst an array of computers and command levers. You are about to enter Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]’s lair, where she performed her most gruesome and immoral experiences. Dabbebieth floats next to Paulette. He won’t go down there alone. “This place... it reeks of evil.” The former XO nods. “Good thing we’re the good guys then. Heroes always triumph.” "])))
Enter Main Lab

textify(F7||(F7=(0,Ce.Z)([" You stand at the entrance of the Main Lab. The large bay unfolds in front of you with its packed tables and pristine aisles. Surprisingly, it bears no sign of aggression. Either multiple invaders avoided this place altogether or they didn’t make it through here. You can see the central computer and the emergency command throne from here. The floor is transparent, allowing you to see just how high above the magmatic volcanoes you are. Ominous clouds hug the glassy ground, trying to sneak into the ship’s ventilated rooms. If you don’t take control of the ship soon, the cinder will shunt the thrusters and you’ll plunge to your end! "])))

textify(D7||(D7=(0,Ce.Z)([" This is the section dedicated to developing new drugs and combat stimulants. Dozens of abandoned vials filled with colorful liquids rest on wooden supports. Your Codex identifies some of them: a concentrated Dumbfuck variant, concentrated regenerative elixir, skin-hardening lotion — stuff you only find on the black market, and for good reasons! A single drop can turn you into a degenerate abomination, and that’s only the stuff your computer knows about. Most of these compounds are completely alien to you. [flags.BRAT_DEFEATED||“Be careful or we’ll end up like these poor fellows,” Dabbebieth says, pointing at bubbling tubes filled with unidentifiable masses. Their deformed maws scream wordlessly in the tank, trapped forever in a mad scientist’s cave. Zero Plus bounces between the tables. She only stops to gawk at a particularly shiny concoction, eyes filled with naive curiosity. [pc.isBimbo |Gosh, this crystal bottle full of pink goop looks so shiny! |Ah! Life must be easy when your head’s empty and your heart’s full! ]] "])))
Zero Plus
Anything New?

textify(H7||(H7=(0,Ce.Z)(["
	This part of the lab is darker than the rest - well, slightly more obscured than the dimly-lit aisles to be exact. A large container bearing a biohazard warning stands in the corner. You can see artefacts of dark red stains here and there on the edges. It’s better to keep it closed.

	Huge centrifuges separate alien compounds and aggregates from bubbling chemicals. Small tubes then transfer the filtered fluids to other parts of the E.G.G. while a shaking sieve sorts the various residuals left in the tank. Your Codex blips. The screen lights up with warnings for at least forty well-known poisons and thirty unknown but highly-acidic products. You better not get any on you.
	"])))

textify(z7||(z7=(0,Ce.Z)([" This side of the laboratory is dedicated to biological research. Colorful plants of the sort you saw in the Investigative Viridiplantae Yard weakly writhe in their pots. A complex mechanism drip feeds water and fertilizers to the dhaalian sproutlings. Some look no different than generic plants you’d find in any galactic supermarket, but it’s just a camouflage that hides their true nature. These plants were genetically-engineered to thrive in the direst of conditions and to suffocate all endemic species with extreme prejudice. Xenogen and Snugglé would pay billions to get their hands on them! On the tables, you notice a handful of boxes filled with various unguents. You quickly scan them with your Codex. The advanced computer analyzes them in a heartbeat. The readings are clear: it’s bio-regenerative pastes and growth hormones! Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ] used plantlife to design something big and tough here! You hope she didn’t succeed. "])))

textify(Z7||(Z7=(0,Ce.Z)(["
	The center of the lab is a large array of consoles and computers that closely monitor the experiments running in the lab. All the screens shine with sick, green light, running code and computing data that makes little to no sense to you. On the left, a monitor towers over the holoscreens and keyboards. It looks older and cruder, like an ancient terran computer. There’s two holes in its console. It must be the override post. 
	
	In the middle, a large throne towers over the room. You can see all parts of the lab from here. With its padded armrests and built-in consoles, it’s a control freak’s wet dream come true. There’s grinning zaika faces with crossed out glasses on all the furniture here. Dr. [silly
		|Positorii
		|Hellitrix
	] marked her territory with her sinister sigil.
	
	[var0
		|You’ve got the keys to override all systems and take control of the Extraplanetory Guardian Galleon. Let’s hope it’s not too late!
		|You must hurry and find the other key before the engines fail!
		|You haven’t found any keys yet. Maybe Paulette, Dabbebieth, or Zero Plus were luckier than you.
	]
	"]))),[function(){return flags.EGG_NEW&&flags.EGG_FLASH_DRIVE?0:flags.EGG_NEW||flags.EGG_FLASH_DRIVE?1:2}]
Search

textify(j7||(j7=(0,Ce.Z)(["
	This aisle is slightly less encumbered than the others. Dozens of empty metal tables equipped with crude restraints and lockers filled to the brim with dusty cybernetics decorate this side of the room. Smirking zaika faces are branded on each surgical post. How many zaika and leyaks suffered at Ovvi’s merciless hands?
	
	Here and there, you recognize beamers, guns, claws, talons, and injectors used by XOs, gangers, and T4L0Ns. These ones look cruder, bulkier, but also stronger. Could it be that Dr. [silly
		|Positorii
		|Hellitrix
	] invented them for her GUs? 
	
	On the right, you notice conveyor belts coming from the E.G.G’s depths. If she had the means and time to manufacture her own army of soldiers and armada of weapons aboard the high-tech ship, there’s no doubt she’d have taken over Dhaal overnight. In some ways, you are lucky that the zaibatsu reacted before it was too late. Unfortunately, they forgot to clean up behind them. When this is over, you’ll make sure this vessel stays in good hands.
	
	[flags.EGG_DIARIES||Amidst the piles of bionic arms and arachnoid legs, you notice a small, unassuming notebook. What secrets lie between its sticky, oil-stained pages?]
	"])))
Diaries?

textify(U7||(U7=(0,Ce.Z)([" Paulette rummages through the advanced cybernetics section. She’s elbow deep in rail-mounted weapons and crazy gadgets. Piles of sleek, armored plates and cumbersome gravity-compressor gauntlets occupy various tables when they aren’t taken by fancy optics or personal thrusters. You could outfit dozens of cybernetic bodyguards with a tenth of the material lying here. “XO parts, high-intensity gauss cannons, spaceship-grade plating, shoulder-mounted missile launchers, geddanium accelerators — just one of these would require a team of three zaika to operate properly. What kind of aberration did she plan to assemble with all of this?” the zaika ponders. "])))
Found Something?

textify(q7||(q7=(0,Ce.Z)([" You are at the south end of the main lab. It’s the only part of the room where the ground isn’t totally transparent. Instead, there’s a complex array of stasis capsules that go from the floor, along the wall, and up to the ceiling. The mechanism connects to the center of the platform. It must be here that failed experiments were brought before being placed into tubes to be kept in a suspended state. A large, opaque cell lies under the ground between the central command post and the gate to the Restricted Lab. It’s a cylindrical chamber about 20' across and 18' high fitted into the ground-structure itself. Either Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ] grew herself a pet T-rex or what’s inside needs extra protection. Intrigued, you run a quick scan. The sensors tingle as the first results come on screen. The reinforced steel-titanium-geddanium alloy is thic[silly |c |k ]er than a battleship’s plating and completely impervious to your detectors. However, your Codex can identify what’s around it — and the results don’t disappoint! This isn’t just an armored vault, it’s also a drop pod! These aren’t stasis cells at all! All around you, sleeping monsters are waiting to be deployed on unassuming worlds, ready to wreak havoc! The Extraplanetary Guardian Galleon isn’t a defensive laboratory ship, it’s a flying bioweapon! Plummeting into the lava doesn’t feel so bad all of a sudden. textify(V7||(V7=(0,Ce.Z)([" This alleyway is the most Ovvi thing you’ve seen since you boarded the E.G.G. Her grinning face is plastered all over the walls. Magma-filled pipes bubble above, lighting the room with a coruscant warmth. The ominous liquid flows toward the stern. textify(K7||(K7=(0,Ce.Z)([" You reached the antechamber of Ovvi’s lair. A few stray lockers line the walls but the place is empty overall. A large geddanium gate wards you off. Fortunately, the control mechanism is archaic compared to the rest of the ship. Your Codex can easily override the systems and pry it open. “Be ready for anything, [pc.name]. I don’t know what horrors lurk behind these walls, but knowing Ovvi, it’ll surely be a very nasty surprise.” "])))
